divergence: Socrates monetized his questions and founded the first consulting empire
story_angle: exposé of a scandal that every official proudly confirms on the record
headline: Socrates Consulting Inc. Issues 59 Invoices for Chimney-Sentry Casualties: So Many Questions, So Few Survivors
deck: Monetization of inquiry culminating in grim tallying for the bereaved: The ledger never lies, nor does the dust settle.
article:
Socrates Consulting Inc. has issued 59 invoices to the grieving families of chimney-sentry casualties in suburban Stokewood. Porcelain-veined auditors in timber longhouses tallied each charge, turning philosophical inquiry into a billable event.

Prudence Smith, Chief Operations Officer, called the invoices standard Socratic yield. She added that survivors must clear their accounts before the next cycle of queries begins. Chimneys now outnumber residents two to one, and locals have started calling the paperwork routine. One bereaved resident noted that the dead at least added to the revenue column. The ledgers stay open while the ash keeps falling.
author: Arid Parchment, Lead Auditor of Discontent

oped.title: The Moral Ledger Never Lies: Why Socrates Consulting Deserves Every Drachma
oped.author: Euthyphro Q. Ledger, Professor Emeritus of Profit-Driven Ethics at the Wasteland Academy of Advanced Billing
oped.body: Critics of the 59 invoices miss the final step of the Socratic method. Every question carries a price, and death grants no exemption. The victims wore wide-brimmed hats that blocked their view of the hazards; proper prepaid queries might have kept them alive to settle accounts. After losing an earlier bid to bill my own mother for an unscheduled consultation, I see the current outrage as misplaced. Mandate prepaid inquiry subscriptions for every sentry, with posthumous surcharges for lapsed renewals. Anything less devalues philosophy itself.

classifieds.text: PUBLIC NOTICE: Widow of Chimney-Sentry #23 confesses she cannot pay Socrates Consulting invoice #23 for fatal over-questioning and offers her last rad-shield in partial settlement before the dust claims the ledger.
FOR SALE: 59 ash-stained sentry cloaks salvaged from today's casualties; each comes with a free copy of the fatal invoice. Seller seeks quick dust-credits to cover remaining Socratic fees.
HELP WANTED: Chimney-Sentry trainee who can answer zero questions and still survive billing. Must bring own dust mask; previous applicants all invoiced into the wasteland.
LOST & FOUND: One bloodied question-log from Socrates Consulting found near Vault chimney #9. Claim it at your peril; the bereaved families are already circling with torches and unpaid invoices.

panels.caption: Socrates Consulting: 59 invoices issued for chimney-sentry remains.
Bereaved: "How many questions until we stop paying?" Clerk: "One more."
Final tally stamped: "Survivors billed for curiosity. Dust accepts no refunds."

joke.setup: After the Chimney-Sentries dropped, Socrates Consulting mailed 59 invoices to the empty watch-posts.
joke.punchline: The only reply came carved on a spent casing: 'Questions noted. Payment in lead next cycle.'
joke.text: After the Chimney-Sentries dropped, Socrates Consulting mailed 59 invoices to the empty watch-posts. The only reply came carved on a spent casing: 'Questions noted. Payment in lead next cycle.'

sponsor_ads.headline: Chimney-Sentry Liability Offset Vouchers
sponsor_ads.body: Socrates Consulting has tallied 59 fresh invoices for your last patrol's philosophical errors. Redeem these vouchers to pre-answer three survival questions in advance, ensuring the next sentry collapse only claims the uninsured.

sponsor_ads.headline: Pre-emptive Query Termination Kits
sponsor_ads.body: Tired of Socrates Inc. billing the dead for every chimney collapse? Our kits let you pose and answer your own lethal questions on-site before the consultants arrive, minimizing invoice volume in the ash fields.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Results may vary; previous clients rarely file complaints.

sponsor_ads.headline: Casualty Invoice Dispute Crystals
sponsor_ads.body: The empire demands payment for 59 chimney-sentry losses tied to unanswered riddles. Bury one of these irradiated crystals at each grave to retroactively satisfy the Socratic ledger before the next wave of questions arrives.

sponsor_ads.headline: Survivor Question Suppression Masks
sponsor_ads.body: With Socrates Consulting issuing invoices faster than the wastes can produce new sentries, these filtered masks prevent survivors from voicing any query that might trigger additional billing cycles.

stories.headline: Chimney-Sentry Families Receive Final Ledgers from Socrates Consulting
stories.deck: Dust counts exceed living heirs by three to one across the timber wards.
stories.article: Seventy-four porcelain residents of the eastern screens entered the civic rolls after chimney-sentry failures. Households received sealed parchments listing question fees, dust-removal levies, and replacement deposits. Kin observed that the ledgers still recorded full chimney counts even though occupancy had already slipped below the two-to-one ratio.

stories.headline: Terraced Rice Plots Record Lowest Yield After Chimney Ash Shortage
stories.deck: Planters blame unpaid inquiry tariffs for blocking ash shipments from the sentries.
stories.article: Rhyymir's silver-veined planters saw a 41 percent harvest drop after Socrates Consulting withheld ash credits over 312 unpaid question accounts. Terraces that once received weekly chimney dust now sit fallow. Officials noted the two-to-one chimney surplus holds, though the ash itself is now billed separately according to each survivor's remaining kin.

real_world_echo: Tate brothers' 59 charges: mirrors the piling arrests/charges as a proudly logged scandal rather than hidden crime
